title: EMERGENCY UTILITY ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: ST VINCENT DE PAUL PORTLAND COUNCIL EMERGENCY UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited, one-time payment assistance for residential electricity, water, or natural gas. Assistance is dependent on available funding and is not guaranteed. Does not provide vouchers for car gasoline, oil, propane, or other fuels. Some areas served may not have appointments. Eligibility: -Customers of Pacific Power, PGE, NW Natural Gas, or municipal water services -Must carefully review all of the information under the website's Get Ready Road page and How to Apply page under Services -Apply no sooner than 3 days before the payment due date or shut-off date -Must have advised the utility company of the need for help -Must have attempted to set up accommodation with utility company (payment plan, extension of due date, medical certificate, etc.) -St Vincent de Paul funds must be enough to stop shut-off or restore service -The person applying must be the named account holder or authorized user on the account -Residential accounts only -One aid payment per year per household Hours: Application Line: Not accepting appointments Information Hotline: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-1:00pm (503) 235-8431 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Information Hotline (503) 235-8431 INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Visit website for information on how to apply and the steps to prepare -Visit website main page www.svdppdx.org often to check the notification banner if applications are open which will note the date, time, and phone number during the time active -Call number provided when application line is open leaving a voicemail with information listed on agency's website page Submission Pass. May have to redial to try to reach the voicemail. -Call for more information. The hotline announces when applications are closed, indicating that appointments are full. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Photo ID card, or combination of non-photo ID documents -Account number -Written utility bill or shut-off notice (digital copy ok) -Signed release of information form (provided by SVdP) -Proof of ability to pay the remainder of minimum due, such as money order made out to utility, or receipt for payment already made (check or cash not valid as proof) AREA SERVED: Clackamas and Multnomah Counties, and zip codes 97062, 97221, and 97225 Services: Electric Service Payment Assistance Gas Service Payment Assistance Water Service Payment Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - PANTRY REFERRAL LINE HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY RENT ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
